Subsection.Text: The definition bénéfices de fabrication et de transformation au Canada in subsection 125.1(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: bénéfices de fabrication et de transformation au Canada

Definition.Text: bénéfices de fabrication et de transformation au Canada En ce qui concerne une société pour une année d’imposition, la partie du total des montants représentant chacun le revenu que la société a tiré pour l’année d’une entreprise exploitée activement au Canada, déterminée en vertu des règles établies à cette fin par règlement pris sur recommandation du ministre des Finances, qui doit s’appliquer à la fabrication ou à la transformation au Canada de marchandises destinées à la vente ou à la location.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs (l)(i) and (ii) of the definition fabrication ou transformation in subsection 125.1(3) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: de la vente ou de la location de marchandises qu’elle a fabriquées ou transformées au Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: de la fabrication ou de la transformation au Canada de marchandises destinées à la vente ou à la location, sauf des marchandises qu’elle devait vendre ou louer elle-même.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2003 and subsequent taxation years.
The definition taxable resource income in subsection 125.11(1) of the Act, as it read immediately before it was repealed by S.C. 2003, c. 28, s. 13(2), is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: taxable resource income, of a taxpayer for a taxation year, is the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the taxpayer’s taxable income for the taxation year exceeds 100/16 of the amount deducted under subsection 125(1) from the taxpayer’s tax otherwise payable under this Part for the year, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is deducted by the taxpayer under paragraph 20(1)(v.1) in computing the taxpayer’s income for the taxation year,
is the percentage that is the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 100% that the number of days in the taxation year that are before 2003 is of the number of days in the taxation year,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 90% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2003 is of the number of days in the taxation year,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 75% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2004 is of the number of days in the taxation year,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 65% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2005 is of the number of days in the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 35% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2006 is of the number of days in the taxation year,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is total of all amounts included in computing the taxpayer’s income for the taxation year under paragraph 59(3.2)(b) or (c),

is the total of all amounts deducted by the taxpayer under any of sections 65 to 66.7, other than subsections 66(4), 66.21(4) and 66.7(2) and (2.3), of this Act, and subsections 17(2) and (6) and section 29 of the Income Tax Application Rules, in computing the taxpayer’s income for the taxation year, and
is 100/16 of the amount deducted under subsection 125(1) from the taxpayer’s tax otherwise payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: The definition taxable resource income in subsection 125.11(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after February 27, 2004.
Subsection (2) applies to taxation years that begin after 2006.
Section 125.2 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011.
Paragraph 125.3(1.1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which its tax payable under this Part (determined without reference to this section) for the year exceeds the amount that would, but for subsections 181.1(4) and 190.1(3), be the total of its taxes payable under Parts I.3 and VI for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011.
The portion of subsection 126(2.22) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Lorsqu’un particulier non-résident dispose, au cours d’une année d’imposition donnée, d’un bien qu’il a acquis la dernière fois à un moment (appelé « moment de l’acquisition » au présent paragraphe) à l’occasion d’une distribution effectuée après le 1er octobre 1996 et à laquelle les alinéas 107(2)a) à c) ne s’appliquent pas par le seul effet du paragraphe 107(5), la fiducie peut déduire de son impôt payable par ailleurs en vertu de la présente partie pour l’année (appelée « année de la distribution » au présent paragraphe) qui comprend le moment de l’acquisition un montant ne dépassant pas le moins élevé des montants suivants :
The portion of paragraph 126(2.22)(a) of the French version of the Act after subparagraph (ii) and before subparagraph (iii) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: s’il est raisonnable de considérer que le montant a été payé sur la partie de tout gain ou bénéfice tiré de la disposition du bien qui s’est accumulée avant la distribution et après le dernier en date des moments ci-après, antérieur à la distribution :

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 126(2.22)(b)(i) and (ii) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: le montant d’impôt en vertu de la présente partie qui était payable par ailleurs par la fiducie pour l’année de la distribution, compte tenu de l’application du présent paragraphe aux dispositions effectuées avant le moment de la disposition,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: le montant de cet impôt qui aurait été payable par la fiducie pour l’année de la distribution si le bien n’avait pas été distribué au particulier.

Subsection.Text: Section 126 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.1):

If a taxpayer is a member of a partnership, any income or profits tax paid to the government of a particular country other than Canada — in respect of the income of the partnership for a period during which the taxpayer’s direct or indirect share of the income of the partnership under the income tax laws (referred to in subsection (4.12) as the “relevant foreign tax law”) of any country other than Canada under the laws of which any income of the partnership is subject to income taxation, is less than the taxpayer’s direct or indirect share of the income for the purposes of this Act — is not included in computing the taxpayer’s business-income tax or non-business-income tax for any taxation year.

For the purposes of subsection (4.11), a taxpayer is not to be considered to have a lesser direct or indirect share of the income of a partnership under the relevant foreign tax law than for the purposes of this Act solely because of one or more of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a difference between the relevant foreign tax law and this Act in the manner of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: computing the income of the partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: allocating the income of the partnership because of the admission to, or withdrawal from, the partnership of any of its members;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the treatment of the partnership as a corporation under the relevant foreign tax law; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that the taxpayer is not treated as a corporation under the relevant foreign tax law.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections (4.11) and (4.12), if a taxpayer is (or is deemed by this subsection to be) a member of a particular partnership that is a member of another partnership, the taxpayer is deemed to be a member of the other partnership.
The description of A in subsection 126(4.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the foreign tax would otherwise be included in business-income tax, the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 26.5% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2011 is of the number of days in the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 25% that the number of days in the taxation year that are after 2011 is of the number of days in the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the foreign tax would otherwise be included in non-business-income tax, the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a corporation that is a Canadian-controlled private corporation throughout the taxation year, that proportion of 28% that the number of days in the taxation year that are after 2010 is of the number of days in the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer is not a Canadian-controlled private corporation throughout the taxation year, the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 16.5% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2011 is of the number of days in the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that proportion of 15% that the number of days in the taxation year that are after 2011 is of the number of days in the taxation year,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 126(4.4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a disposition or acquisition of property deemed to be made by subsection 10(12) or (13), 14(14) or (15) or 45(1), section 70, 128.1 or 132.2, subsections 138(11.3) or 142.5(2), paragraph 142.6(1)(b) or subsections 142.6(1.1) or (1.2) or 149(10) is not a disposition or acquisition, as the case may be; and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 126(5)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount obtained by multiplying the taxpayer’s income from the business in the taxing country for the year by the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: that proportion of 26.5% that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2011 is of the number of days in the taxation year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: that proportion of 25% that the number of days in the taxation year that are after 2011 is of the number of days in the taxation year

Subsection.Text: Subsection 126(6) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if, in computing a taxpayer’s income for a taxation year from a business carried on by the taxpayer in Canada, an amount is included in respect of interest paid or payable to the taxpayer by a person resident in a country other than Canada, and the taxpayer has paid to the government of that other country a non-business income tax for the year with respect to the amount, the amount is, in applying the definition qualifying incomes in subsection (7) for the purpose of subsection (1), deemed to be income from a source in that other country.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition business-income tax in subsection 126(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: impôt sur le revenu tiré d’une entreprise

Definition.Text: business-income tax paid by a taxpayer for a taxation year in respect of businesses carried on by the taxpayer in a country other than Canada (referred to in this definition as the “business country”) means, subject to subsections (4.1) to (4.2), the portion of any income or profits tax paid by the taxpayer for the year to the government of a country other than Canada that can reasonably be regarded as tax in respect of the income of the taxpayer from a business carried on by the taxpayer in the business country, but does not include a tax, or the portion of a tax, that can reasonably be regarded as relating to an amount that

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition non-business-income tax in subsection 126(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: impôt sur le revenu ne provenant pas d’une entreprise

Definition.Text: non-business-income tax paid by a taxpayer for a taxation year to the government of a country other than Canada means, subject to subsections (4.1) to (4.2), the portion of any income or profits tax paid by the taxpayer for the year to the government of that country that

Subsection.Text: Subsections (4), (9) and (10) apply to income or profits tax paid for taxation years of a taxpayer that end after March 4, 2010, except that, for taxation years of the taxpayer that end on or before August 27, 2010,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 126(4.11) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4), is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: If a taxpayer is a member of a partnership, any income or profits tax paid to the government of a particular country other than Canada — in respect of the income of the partnership for a period during which the taxpayer’s share of the income of the partnership under the income tax laws (referred to in subsection (4.12) as the “relevant foreign tax law”) of any country other than Canada under the laws of which the income of the partnership is subject to income taxation, is less than the taxpayer’s share of the income for the purposes of this Act — is not included in computing the taxpayer’s business-income tax or non-business-income tax for any taxation year.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 126(4.12) of the Act before paragraph (a), as enacted by subsection (4), is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (4.11), a taxpayer is not to be considered to have a lesser share of the income of a partnership under the relevant foreign tax law than for the purposes of this Act solely because of one or more of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 126 of the Act is to be read without reference to its subsection (4.13), as enacted by subsection (4).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (5) and (7) apply to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011.

Subsection (6) applies to dispositions and acquisitions that occur after 1998, except that, in applying paragraph 126(4.4)(a) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (6), to dispositions and acquisitions that occur before June 28, 1999, that paragraph is to be read without reference to “10(12) or (13), 14(14) or (15), or”.
Subsection (8) applies to amounts received after February 27, 2004.
Section 126.1 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies in respect of forms filed after March 20, 2003.
Paragraphs 127(1)(a) and (b) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: les 2/3 de tout impôt sur les opérations forestières, payé par le contribuable au gouvernement d’une province sur le revenu pour l’année tiré d’opérations forestières dans cette province;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: 6 2/3 % du revenu du contribuable pour l’année, tiré d’opérations forestières dans la province, dont fait mention l’alinéa a).

Subsection.Text: The definition revenu pour l’année tiré des opérations forestières dans la province in subsection 127(2) of the French version of the Act is repealed.
The definition impôt sur les opérations forestières in subsection 127(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: impôt sur les opérations forestières Impôt levé par la législature d’une province et qui est, par règlement, déclaré être un impôt d’application générale sur le revenu tiré d’opérations forestières.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127(2) of the French version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: revenu pour l’année tiré d’opérations forestières dans la province

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: income for the year from logging operations in the province

Definition.Text: revenu pour l’année tiré d’opérations forestières dans la province S’entend au sens du règlement.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 127(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Contributions to registered parties and candidates
Subsection.Text: There may be deducted from the tax otherwise payable by a taxpayer under this Part for a taxation year in respect of the total of all amounts each of which is the eligible amount of a monetary contribution that is referred to in the Canada Elections Act and that is made by the taxpayer in the year to a registered party, a registered association or a candidate, as those terms are defined in that Act,
Subsection 127(4.2) of the Act, as it read immediately before it was repealed by S.C. 2006, c. 9, s. 64(2), is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Allocation of amount contributed among partners
Subsection.Text: If at the end of a fiscal period of a partnership a taxpayer is a member of the partnership, the taxpayer’s share of the total that would, if the partnership were a person and its fiscal period were its taxation year, be the total referred to in subsection (3) in respect of the partnership for that taxation year is deemed for the purpose of that subsection to be a monetary contribution made by the taxpayer in the taxpayer’s taxation year in which the fiscal period of the partnership ends.
Subsection 127(4.2) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (6), is repealed.
The definition eligible salary and wages in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: eligible salary and wages payable by a taxpayer to an eligible apprentice means the amount, if any, that is the salary and wages payable by the taxpayer to the eligible apprentice in respect of the first 24 months of the apprenticeship (other than a qualified expenditure incurred by the taxpayer in a taxation year, remuneration that is based on profits, bonuses, amounts described in section 6 or 7, and amounts deemed to be incurred by subsection 78(4));

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition pre-production mining expenditure in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not an expense that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was renounced under subsection 66(12.6) to the taxable Canadian corporation except if the corporation is, on the effective date of the renunciation,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a corporation that would be a principal business corporation, as defined in subsection 66(15), if that definition were read without reference to its paragraphs (a), (a.1), (f), (h) and (i), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the sole shareholder of the corporation that renounced the expenditure, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is a member’s share of an expense incurred by a partnership unless the expense was deemed by subsection 66(18) to have been made or incurred at the end of the fiscal period of the partnership by the member and throughout the fiscal period of the partnership in which the expense was incurred

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: each member of the partnership would (otherwise than because of being a member of the partnership) be a “principal-business corporation” as defined in subsection 66(15) of the Act, if that definition were read without reference to its paragraphs (a), (a.1), (f), (h) and (i), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the corporation is a member of the partnership at the time the expenditure is incurred and would not be a specified member of the partnership if the definition specified member in subsection 248(1) were read without reference to its subparagraph (b)(ii),

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 127(27)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the cost, or a portion of the cost, of the particular property was a qualified expenditure, or would if this Act were read without reference to subsection (26) be a qualified expenditure, to the taxpayer,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the cost, or the portion of the cost, of the particular property is included, or would if this Act were read without reference to subsection (26) be included, in an amount, a percentage of which can reasonably be considered to be included in computing the taxpayer’s investment tax credit at the end of the taxation year, and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 127(27) of the Act after paragraph (d) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: there shall be added to the taxpayer’s tax otherwise payable under this Part for the year the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that can reasonably be considered to be included in the taxpayer’s investment tax credit at the end of any taxation year, or that would be so included if this Act were read without reference to subsection (26), in respect of the particular property, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that is the percentage — that is the sum of each percentage described in paragraph (c) that has been applied to compute the taxpayer’s investment tax credit in respect of the particular property — of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case where the particular property or the other property is disposed of to a person who deals at arm’s length with the taxpayer,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the proceeds of disposition of the property, if the property

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: is the particular property and is neither first term shared-use equipment nor second term shared-use equipment, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: is the other property,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: 25% of the proceeds of disposition of the property, if the property is the particular property, is first term shared-use equipment and is not second term shared-use equipment, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: 50% of the proceeds of disposition of the property, if the property is the particular property and is second term shared-use equipment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case where the particular property or the other property is converted to commercial use or is disposed of to a person who does not deal at arm’s length with the taxpayer,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the fair market value of the property, if the property

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: is the particular property and is neither first term shared-use equipment nor second term shared-use equipment, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: is the other property,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: 25% of the fair market value of the property at the time of its conversion or disposition, if the particular property is first term shared-use equipment and is not second term shared-use equipment, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: 50% of the fair market value of the property at the time of its conversion or disposition, if the particular property is second term shared-use equipment.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (5) applies to monetary contributions made after December 20, 2002, except that, for monetary contributions made before 2004, the reference to “to a registered party, a registered association or a candidate” in subsection 127(3) of the Act, as amended by subsection (5), is to be read as a reference to “to a registered party or a candidate”.
Subsection (6) applies to monetary contributions made after December 20, 2002 and before 2007.
Subsection (7) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2007, except that it does not apply in respect of monetary contributions made before that day.
Subsection (8) applies to taxation years that end after November 5, 2010.
Subsection (9) applies to the 2003 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsections (10) and (11) apply to dispositions and conversions that occur after December 20, 2002.
Paragraph (b) of the definition approved share in subsection 127.4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a share issued by a prescribed labour-sponsored venture capital corporation that is not a registered labour-sponsored venture capital corporation if, at the time of the issue, no province under the laws (described in section 6701 of the Income Tax Regulations) of which the corporation is registered or established provides assistance in respect of the acquisition of the share;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.4(6) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and’’ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: nil, if the share is issued in exchange for another share of the corporation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to acquisitions of shares that occur after 2003.
Subsection (2) applies to the 2004 and subsequent taxation years.
The portion of subparagraph 127.52(1)(d)(ii) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: each amount that is designated by a trust for a particular year of the trust in respect of the individual and deemed by subsection 104(21) to be a taxable capital gain for the year of the individual were equal to the amount obtained by the formula

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: this Act were read without reference to subsection 104(21.6);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.52(1)(d) of the Act, as amended by subsections (1) and (2), is amended by adding “and” at the end of subparagraph (i), by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by repealing subparagraph (iii).
Paragraph 127.52(1)(e) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (i) and by adding the following after subparagraph (i):

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual’s income for the year from property, or from the business of selling the product of property, described in Class 43.1 or 43.2 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations, determined before deducting those amounts, and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amounts deducted under any of subsections 110(2), 110.6(2), (2.1), (2.2) and (12) and 110.7(1),

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) apply to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011.
Subsection (2) applies to the 2000 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection (4) applies in respect of taxation years that end after 2008.
Subparagraph 128(2)(g)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual’s unused tuition, textbook and education tax credits (as determined under subsection 118.61(1)) at the end of the last taxation year that ended before that time is deemed to be nil;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2006 and subsequent taxation years.
The portion of subsection 128.1(5) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

If an individual is deemed by subsection (4) to have disposed of a property in a taxation year, in applying sections 155 and 156 and subsections 156.1(1) to (3) and 161(2), (4) and (4.01) and any regulations made for the purposes of those provisions, the individual’s total tax payable under this Part for the year is deemed to be the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual’s total tax payable under this Part for the year, determined before taking into consideration the specified future tax consequences for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 128.1(7)(b) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: est propriétaire, à ce moment, d’un bien qu’il a acquis, la dernière fois, à l’occasion d’une distribution à laquelle le paragraphe 107(2) se serait appliqué, n’eût été le paragraphe 107(5), effectuée par une fiducie à un moment (appelé « moment de la distribution » au présent paragraphe) postérieur au 1er octobre 1996 et antérieur au moment donné;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 128.1(7)(d) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: sous réserve des alinéas e) et f), si le particulier et la fiducie en font conjointement le choix dans un document présenté au ministre au plus tard à la première en date des dates d’échéance de production qui leur est applicable pour leur année d’imposition qui comprend le moment donné, le paragraphe 107(2.1) ne s’applique pas à la distribution pour ce qui est des biens que le particulier a acquis à l’occasion de la distribution et qui étaient des biens canadiens imposables lui appartenant tout au long de la période ayant commencé au moment de la distribution et se terminant au moment donné;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 128.1(7)(e)(i) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: il résidait au Canada au moment de la distribution,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 128.1(7)(f)(i) and (ii) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: malgré l’alinéa 107(2.1)a), la fiducie est réputée avoir disposé du bien au moment de la distribution pour un produit de disposition égal au total des montants suivants :

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: le coût indiqué du bien pour elle immédiatement avant ce moment,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: l’excédent du montant de la réduction prévue au paragraphe 40(3.7) et dont il est question à l’alinéa e), sur le moins élevé des montants suivants :

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: le coût indiqué du bien pour la fiducie immédiatement avant le moment de la distribution,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: le montant que le particulier et la fiducie ont indiqué conjointement pour l’application du présent alinéa dans le document concernant le choix prévu à l’alinéa d) relativement au bien,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: malgré l’alinéa 107(2.1)b), le particulier est réputé avoir acquis le bien au moment de la distribution à un coût égal à l’excédent du montant déterminé par ailleurs selon l’alinéa 107(2)b) sur le montant de la réduction prévue au paragraphe 40(3.7) et dont il est question à l’alinéa e), ou, s’il est moins élevé, le montant indiqué selon la subdivision (i)(B)(II);

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 128.1(7)(g) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: g)
Paragraph.Text: si le particulier et la fiducie en font conjointement le choix, dans un document présenté au ministre au plus tard à la dernière en date des dates d’échéance de production qui leur est applicable pour leur année d’imposition qui comprend le moment donné, relativement à chaque bien dont le particulier a été propriétaire tout au long de la période ayant commencé au moment de la distribution et se terminant au moment donné et dont il est réputé, par l’alinéa (1)b), avoir disposé du fait qu’il est devenu un résident du Canada, le produit de disposition pour la fiducie, selon l’alinéa 107(2.1)a), au moment de la distribution et le coût d’acquisition du bien pour le particulier au moment donné sont réputés, malgré les alinéas 107(2.1)a) et b), correspondre à ce produit et à ce coût, déterminés compte non tenu du présent alinéa, diminués du moins élevé des montants suivants :

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 128.1(7)(i) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: i)
Paragraph.Text: malgré les paragraphes 152(4) à (5), le ministre établit, pour tenir compte des choix prévus au présent paragraphe, toute cotisation concernant l’impôt payable par la fiducie ou le particulier en vertu de la présente loi pour toute année qui est antérieure à l’année comprenant le moment donné sans être antérieure à l’année comprenant le moment de la distribution; pareille cotisation est toutefois sans effet sur le calcul des montants suivants :

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011.
Section 128.3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If, in a transaction to which section 51, subparagraphs 85.1(1)(a)(i) and (ii), subsection 85.1(8) or section 86 or 87 applies, a person acquires a share (in this section referred to as the “new share”) in exchange for another share or equity in a SIFT wind-up entity (in this section referred to as the “old share”), for the purposes of section 119, subsections 126(2.21) to (2.23), subparagraph 128.1(4)(b)(iv) and subsections 128.1(6) to (8), 180.1(1.4) and 220(4.5) and (4.6), the person is deemed not to have disposed of the old share, and the new share is deemed to be the same share as the old share.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2001, except that, before December 20, 2007, section 128.3 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read as follows:

Section.Text: If, in a transaction to which section 51, subparagraphs 85.1(1)(a)(i) and (ii) or section 86 or 87 applies, a person acquires a share (in this section referred to as the “new share”) in exchange for another share (in this section referred to as the “old share”), for the purposes of section 119, subsections 126(2.21) to (2.23), subparagraph 128.1(4)(b)(iv) and subsections 128.1(6) to (8), 180.1(1.4) and 220(4.5) and (4.6), the person is deemed not to have disposed of the old share, and the new share is deemed to be the same share as the old share.

Subsection.Text: Clauses 129(3)(a)(ii)(B) and (C) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: 100/35 of the total of amounts deducted under subsection 126(1) from its tax for the year otherwise payable under this Part, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the amount determined by multiplying the total of amounts deducted under subsection 126(2) from its tax for the year otherwise payable under this Part, by the relevant factor for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 129(3)(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation’s tax for the year payable under this Part,

Subsection.Text: Clause 129(3)(a)(ii)(B) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011.
Clause 129(3)(a)(ii)(C) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to the 2003 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection (2) applies to taxation years that begin after 2007.
Paragraph 130.1(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, if an amount is received by a taxpayer in a taxation year as, on account of, in lieu of payment of or in satisfaction of, the dividend, the amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shall not be included in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year as income from a share of the capital stock of the corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is deemed to be a capital gain of the taxpayer from the disposition of capital property in the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 130.1(4.2) to (4.5) of the Act are repealed.
Subparagraph 130.1(6)(f)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: debts owing to the corporation that were secured, whether by mortgages, hypothecs or in any other manner, on houses (as defined in section 2 of the National Housing Act) or on property included within a housing project (as defined in that section as it read on June 16, 1999), and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011, except that if any part of a dividend declared by a corporation is in respect of capital gains of the corporation from dispositions of property that occurred before October 18, 2000, then paragraph 130.1(4)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read, in its application to that part of the dividend, as it read in its application to the corporation’s last taxation year that began before November 1, 2011.
Subsection (3) applies to property acquired by a corporation after October 31, 2011, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property is a particular debt

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is owing to the corporation and secured on property (referred to in this paragraph as the “subject property”),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that replaces a debt (referred to in this paragraph as the “old debt”) that was on October 31, 2011 owing to the corporation and secured on the subject property, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: that has a maximum term for repayment that does not exceed the maximum term for repayment, in effect on October 31, 2011, of the old debt; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation would be a mortgage investment corporation for its taxation year that includes October 31, 2011 if that taxation year were determined as though it ended on October 31, 2011.

Subsection.Text: If property that is held by a corporation on October 31, 2011 consists of debt, the term for repayment of the debt is extended by agreement entered into on a particular date that is after October 31, 2011, and the extended term exceeds the maximum term for repayment of the debt in effect on October 31, 2011, then the property is deemed, for the purposes of applying subsection (5), to have been acquired by the corporation on the particular date.
Paragraph 131(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notwithstanding any other provision of this Act (other than paragraph (5.1)(b)), if an amount is received by a taxpayer in a taxation year as, on account of, in lieu of payment of or in satisfaction of, the dividend, the amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shall not be included in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year as income from a share of the capital stock of the corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is deemed to be a capital gain of the taxpayer from the disposition of capital property in the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 131(1.5) to (1.9) of the Act are repealed.
Subparagraph 131(5.1)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (1)(b)(ii) does not apply in respect of the dividend, to the extent of the TCP gains distribution, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition capital gains dividend account in subsection 131(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: its capital gains, for all taxation years that began more than 60 days before that time, from dispositions of property after 1971 and before that time while it was a mutual fund corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts each of which is an amount in respect of a distribution made by a trust to the corporation, at a time that is after its 2004 taxation year and at which it is a mutual fund corporation, in respect of capital gains of the trust equal to twice the amount determined by the following formula:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the distribution, and
is the amount designated under subsection 104(21) by the trust in respect of the net taxable capital gains of the trust attributable to those capital gains

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(iii) of the definition capital gains dividend account in subsection 131(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount equal to 100/14 of its capital gains refund for any taxation year throughout which it was a mutual fund corporation where the year ended more than 60 days before that time;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) and (5) apply to taxation years that begin after October 31, 2011, except that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if any part of a dividend declared by a corporation is in respect of capital gains of the corporation from dispositions of property that occurred before October 18, 2000, then paragraph 131(1)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read, in its application to that part of the dividend, as it read in its application to the corporation’s last taxation year that began before November 1, 2011; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a corporation had a capital gains refund for a taxation year that began before November 2011, then in computing the capital gains dividend account of the corporation at any time in a taxation year of the corporation that begins after October 31, 2011, subparagraph (b)(iii) of the definition capital gains dividend account in subsection 131(6) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (5), is to be read, in its application to the corporation, as it read in its application to the corporation’s last taxation year that began before November 1, 2011.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (4) applies to the 2005 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph 132(6)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it complied with prescribed conditions.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2000 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph 132.11(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the trust’s taxation year ends on December 15 because of paragraph (a), subject to subsection (1.1), each subsequent taxation year of the trust is deemed to be the period that begins at the beginning of December 16 of a calendar year and ends at the end of December 15 of the following calendar year or at any earlier time that is determined under paragraph 132.2(3)(b) or subsection 142.6(1); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 132.11(1)(c) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: chacun de ses exercices qui soit commence dans une de ses années d’imposition se terminant le 15 décembre par l’effet de l’alinéa a), soit se termine dans une de ses années d’imposition ultérieures, doit prendre fin au plus tard à la fin de l’année où il a commencé.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 132.11(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amounts paid or payable to beneficiaries
Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsection 104(24), for the purposes of subsections (5) and (6) and 104(6) and (13) and paragraph (i) of the definition disposition in subsection 248(1) each amount that is paid, or that becomes payable, by a trust to a beneficiary after the end of a particular taxation year of the trust that ends on December 15 of a calendar year because of subsection (1) and before the end of that calendar year, is deemed to have been paid or to have become payable, as the case may be, to the beneficiary at the end of the particular year and not at any other time.

Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 1999, except that, in applying paragraph 132.11(1)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), to taxation years that end before 2000, that paragraph is to be read without reference to “subject to subsection (1.1)”.
Subsection (2) applies to the 1998 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection (3) applies to amounts that, after 1999, are paid or have become payable by a trust.
Section 132.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Definitions re qualifying exchange of mutual funds

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: first post-exchange year, of a fund in respect of a qualifying exchange, means the taxation year of the fund that begins immediately after the acquisition time.

qualifying exchange means a transfer at any time (in this section referred to as the “transfer time”) of all or substantially all of the property of a mutual fund corporation (other than a SIFT wind-up corporation) or a mutual fund trust to a mutual fund trust (in this section referred to as the “transferor” and “transferee”, respectively, and as the “funds”) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all or substantially all of the shares issued by the transferor and outstanding immediately before the transfer time are within 60 days after the transfer time disposed of to the transferor;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no person disposing of shares of the transferor to the transferor within that 60-day period (otherwise than pursuant to the exercise of a statutory right of dissent) receives any consideration for the shares other than units of the transferee; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the funds jointly so elect, by filing a prescribed form with the Minister on or before the election’s due date.

Definition.Text: share means a share of the capital stock of a mutual fund corporation and a unit of a mutual fund trust.

Subsection.Text: In respect of a qualifying exchange, a time referred to in the following list immediately follows the time that precedes it in the list

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer time;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the first intervening time;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the acquisition time;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the beginning of the funds’ first post-exchange years;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the depreciables disposition time;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the second intervening time; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the depreciables acquisition time.

Subsection.Text: In respect of a qualifying exchange,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each property of a fund, other than property disposed of by the transferor to the transferee at the transfer time and depreciable property, is deemed to have been disposed of, and to have been reacquired by the fund, at the first intervening time, for an amount equal to the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value of the property at the transfer time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the greater of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: its cost amount, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount that the fund designates in respect of the property in a notification to the Minister accompanying the election in respect of the qualifying exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (l), the last taxation years of the funds that began before the transfer time are deemed to have ended at the acquisition time, and their first post-exchange years are deemed to have begun immediately after those last taxation years ended;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each depreciable property of a fund (other than property to which subsection (5) applies and property to which paragraph (d) would, if this Act were read without reference to this paragraph, apply) is deemed to have been disposed of, and to have been reacquired, by the fund at the second intervening time for an amount equal to the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value of the property at the depreciables disposition time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the greater of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the lesser of its capital cost and its cost amount to the disposing fund at the depreciables disposition time, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount that the fund designates in respect of the property in a notification to the Minister accompanying the election in respect of the qualifying exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if at the second intervening time the undepreciated capital cost to a fund of depreciable property of a prescribed class exceeds the fair market value of all the property of that class, the excess is to be deducted in computing the fund’s income for the taxation year that includes the transfer time and is deemed to have been allowed in respect of property of that class under regulations made for the purpose of paragraph 20(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: except as provided in paragraph (m), the transferor’s cost of any particular property received by the transferor from the transferee as consideration for the disposition of the property is deemed to be

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: nil, if the particular property is a unit of the transferee, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular property’s fair market value at the transfer time, in any other case;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor’s proceeds of disposition of any units of the transferee that were disposed of by the transferor at any particular time that is within 60 days after the transfer time in exchange for shares of the transferor, are deemed to be equal to the cost amount of the units to the transferor immediately before the particular time;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if, at any particular time that is within 60 days after the transfer time, a taxpayer disposes of shares of the transferor to the transferor in exchange for units of the transferee

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition of the shares and the cost to the taxpayer of the units are deemed to be equal to the cost amount to the taxpayer of the shares immediately before the particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of applying section 116 in respect of the disposition, the shares are deemed to be excluded property of the taxpayer,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the qualifying exchange occurs after 2004, for the purposes of applying section 218.3 in respect of that exchange, the payment or crediting of the units to the taxpayer by the transferor is deemed not to be an assessable distribution,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: where all of the taxpayer’s shares of the transferor have been so disposed of, for the purpose of applying section 39.1 in respect of the taxpayer after that disposition, the transferee is deemed to be the same entity as the transferor,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of the definition designated beneficiary in section 210, the units are deemed not to have been held at any time by the transferor, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: where the taxpayer is at the particular time affiliated with one or both of the funds,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: those units are deemed not to be identical to any other units of the transferee,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the taxpayer is the transferee, and the units cease to exist when the taxpayer acquires them (or, for greater certainty, when the taxpayer would but for that cessation have acquired them), the taxpayer is deemed

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: to have acquired those units at the particular time, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: to have disposed of those units immediately after the particular time for proceeds of disposition equal to the cost amount to the taxpayer of those units at the particular time, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: in any other case, for the purpose of computing any gain or loss of the taxpayer from the taxpayer’s first disposition, after the particular time, of each of those units,

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if that disposition is a renunciation or surrender of the unit by the taxpayer for no consideration, and is not in favour of any person other than the transferee, the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition of that unit are deemed to be equal to that unit’s cost amount to the taxpayer immediately before that disposition, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if subclause (I) does not apply, the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition of that unit are deemed to be equal to the greater of that unit’s fair market value and its cost amount to the taxpayer immediately before that disposition;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: where a share to which paragraph (g) applies would, if this Act were read without reference to this paragraph, cease to be a qualified investment (within the meaning assigned by subsection 146(1), 146.1(1) or 146.3(1), section 204 or subsection 205(1) or 207.01(1)) as a consequence of the qualifying exchange, the share is deemed to be a qualified investment until the earlier of the day that is 60 days after the day that includes the transfer time and the time at which it is disposed of in accordance with paragraph (g);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be added to the amount determined under the description of A in the definition refundable capital gains tax on hand in subsection 132(4) in respect of the transferee for its taxation years that begin after the transfer time the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferor’s refundable capital gains tax on hand (within the meaning assigned by subsection 131(6) or 132(4), as the case may be) at the end of its taxation year that includes the transfer time

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferor’s capital gains refund (within the meaning assigned by paragraph 131(2)(a) or 132(1)(a), as the case may be) for that year;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: no amount in respect of a non-capital loss, net capital loss, restricted farm loss, farm loss or limited partnership loss of a fund for a taxation year that began before the transfer time is deductible in computing the taxable income of either of the funds for a taxation year that begins after the transfer time;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: if the transferor is a mutual fund trust, for the purposes of subsections 132.1(1) and (3) to (5), the transferee is deemed after the transfer time to be the same mutual fund trust as, and a continuation of, the transferor;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: if the transferor is a mutual fund corporation

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of subsection 131(4) but, for greater certainty, without having any effect on the computation of any amount determined under this Part, the transferor is deemed in respect of any share disposed of in accordance with paragraph (g) to be a mutual fund corporation at the time of the disposition, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of Part I.3 but, for greater certainty, without having any effect on the computation of any amount determined under this Part, the transferor’s taxation year that, if this Act were read without reference to this paragraph, would have included the transfer time is deemed to have ended immediately before the transfer time;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of determining the funds’ capital gains redemptions (as defined in subsection 131(6) or 132(4), as the case may be), for their taxation years that include the transfer time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of the cost amounts to the transferor of all its properties at the end of the year is deemed to be the total of all amounts each of which is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the transferor’s proceeds of disposition of a property that was transferred to a transferee on the qualifying exchange, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the cost amount to the transferor at the end of the year of a property that was not transferred on the qualifying exchange, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferee is deemed not to have acquired any property that was transferred to it on the qualifying exchange; and

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: except as provided in subparagraph (l)(i), the transferor is, notwithstanding subsections 131(8) and 132(6), deemed to be neither a mutual fund corporation nor a mutual fund trust for taxation years that begin after the transfer time.

Subsection.Text: If a transferor transfers a property, other than a depreciable property, to a transferee in a qualifying exchange,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee is deemed to have acquired the property at the acquisition time and not to have acquired the property at the transfer time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor’s proceeds of disposition of the property and the transferee’s cost of the property are deemed to be the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value of the property at the transfer time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the greatest of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the cost amount to the transferor of the property at the transfer time,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount that the funds agree on in respect of the property in their election, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the fair market value at the transfer time of the consideration (other than units of the transferee) received by the transferor for the disposition of the property.

Subsection.Text: If a transferor transfers a depreciable property to a transferee in a qualifying exchange,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is deemed to have disposed of the property at the depreciables disposition time, and not to have disposed of the property at the transfer time;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee is deemed to have acquired the property at the depreciables acquisition time, and not to have acquired the property at the transfer time;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor’s proceeds of disposition of the property and the transferee’s cost of the property are deemed to be the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value of the property at the transfer time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the greatest of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the lesser of its capital cost and its cost amount to the transferor immediately before the depreciables disposition time,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount that the funds agree on in respect of the property in their election, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the fair market value at the transfer time of the consideration (other than units of the transferee) received by the transferor for the disposition of the property;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: where the capital cost of the property to the transferor exceeds the transferor’s proceeds of disposition of the property under paragraph (c), for the purposes of sections 13 and 20 and any regulations made for the purpose of paragraph 20(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property’s capital cost to the transferee is deemed to be the amount that was its capital cost to the transferor, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the excess is deemed to have been allowed to the transferee in respect of the property under regulations made for the purpose of paragraph 20(1)(a) in computing income for taxation years ending before the transfer time; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: where two or more depreciable properties of a prescribed class are disposed of by the transferor to the transferee in the same qualifying exchange, paragraph (c) applies as if each property so disposed of had been separately disposed of in the order designated by the transferor at the time of making the election in respect of the qualifying exchange or, if the transferor does not so designate any such order, in the order designated by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: The due date of an election referred to in paragraph (c) of the definition qualifying exchange in subsection (1) is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is six months after the day that includes the transfer time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on joint application by the funds, any later day that the Minister accepts.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on joint application by the funds on or before the due date of an election referred to in paragraph (c) of the definition qualifying exchange in subsection (1), grant permission to amend or revoke the election.

The definitions first post-exchange year and share in subsection 132.2(1) and subsections 132.2(2) to (5) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), apply to qualifying exchanges that occur after 1998, except that,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a qualifying exchange occurred before July 18, 2005 and the transferee has, before that day, filed a return of income, for any taxation year, that identified the realization of any loss that would not have been realized if paragraphs 132.2(3)(f) and (g) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), had applied in respect of the qualifying exchange, those paragraphs are to be read in their application to the qualifying exchange as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor’s proceeds of disposition of any units of the transferee that were received by the transferor as consideration for the disposition of the property, and that were disposed of by the transferor within 60 days after the transfer time in exchange for shares of the transferor, are deemed to be nil;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if, within 60 days after the transfer time, a taxpayer disposes of shares of the transferor to the transferor in exchange for units of the transferee

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition of the shares and the cost to the taxpayer of the units are deemed to be equal to the cost amount to the taxpayer of the shares immediately before the transfer time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of applying section 116 in respect of the disposition, the shares are deemed to be excluded property of the taxpayer,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the qualifying exchange occurs after 2004, for the purposes of applying section 218.3 in respect of that exchange, the payment or crediting of the units to the taxpayer by the transferor is deemed not to be an assessable distribution,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: where all of the taxpayer’s shares of the transferor have been so disposed of, for the purpose of applying section 39.1 in respect of the taxpayer after that disposition, the transferee is deemed to be the same entity as the transferor, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of the definition designated beneficiary in section 210, the units are deemed not to have been held at any time by the transferor;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: before the 2008 taxation year, paragraph 132.2(3)(h) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: where a share to which paragraph (g) applies would, if this Act were read without reference to this paragraph, cease to be a qualified investment (within the meaning assigned by subsection 146(1), 146.1(1) or 146.3(1) or section 204) as a consequence of the qualifying exchange, the share is deemed to be a qualified investment until the earlier of the day that is 60 days after the transfer time and the time at which it is disposed of in accordance with paragraph (g);

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the 2008 taxation year, paragraph 132.2(3)(h) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: where a share to which paragraph (g) applies would, if this Act were read without reference to this paragraph, cease to be a qualified investment (within the meaning assigned by subsection 146(1), 146.1(1) or 146.3(1), section 204 or subsection 205(1)) as a consequence of the qualifying exchange, the share is deemed to be a qualified investment until the earlier of the day that is 60 days after the transfer time and the time at which it is disposed of in accordance with paragraph (g);

Subsection.Text: For qualifying exchanges that occurred after June 1994 and before 1999, paragraph 132.2(1)(j) of the Act is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: where shares of the transferor have been disposed of by a taxpayer to the transferor in exchange for units of the transferee within 60 days after the transfer time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition of the shares and the cost to the taxpayer of the units are deemed to be equal to the cost amount to the taxpayer of the shares immediately before the transfer time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if all of the taxpayer’s shares of the transferor have been so disposed of, for the purposes of applying section 39.1 in respect of the taxpayer after that disposition, the transferee is deemed to be the same entity as the transferor, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of the definition designated beneficiary in section 210, the units are deemed not to have been held at any time by the transferor;

Subsection.Text: For qualifying exchanges that occurred after June 1994 and before 1999, subsection 132.2(1) of the Act is to be read as if it contained a paragraph (j.1) that read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (j.1)
Paragraph.Text: if shares of the transferor have been disposed of by a taxpayer to the transferor in exchange for units of the transferee within 60 days after the transfer time, for the purposes of applying section 116 in respect of the disposition, the shares are deemed to be excluded property of the taxpayer;

Subsection.Text: The definition qualifying exchange in subsection 132.2(1) and subsections 132.2(6) and (7) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), apply to transfers that occur after June 1994, except that, before December 20, 2007, the portion of the definition qualifying exchange in subsection 132.2(1) before paragraph (a), as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read as follows:

Definition.Text: qualifying exchange means a transfer at any time (in this section referred to as the “transfer time”) of all or substantially all of the property of a mutual fund corporation or a mutual fund trust to a mutual fund trust (in this section referred to as the “transferor” and “transferee”, respectively, and as the “funds”) if

Subsection.Text: If an election under paragraph (c) of the definition qualifying exchange in subsection 132.2(2) of the Act was made, the election continues to have the effect of having section 132.2 of the Act, as modified from time to time, apply to the transfer.

If an election under subsection 159(4) of the Income Tax Amendments Act, 1997, S.C. 1998, c. 19, was made in respect of a transfer to read subsection 132.2(1) of the Income Tax Act without reference to paragraph 132.2(1)(p) of that Act, the election is, on the application of subsection (1), deemed to have the effect of reading subsection 132.2(3) of that Act, as enacted by subsection (1), in respect of the transfer without reference to its paragraph (i).
Subsection 134.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of applying subsections 104(10) and (11) and 133(6) to (9) (other than the definition non-resident-owned investment corporation in subsection 133(8)), section 212 and any tax treaty, a corporation described in subsection (1) is deemed to be a non-resident-owned investment corporation in its first non-NRO year in respect of dividends paid in that year on shares of its capital stock to a non-resident person, to a trust for the benefit of non-resident persons or their unborn issue or to a non-resident-owned investment corporation.

Subsection (1) applies to a corporation that ceases to be a non-resident-owned investment corporation because of a transaction or an event that occurs, or a circumstance that arises, in a taxation year of the corporation that ends after February 27, 2000.
Subsection 135.1(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person or partnership (in this subsection referred to as the “redeeming entity”) that redeems, acquires or cancels a shareholder’s share shall withhold and forthwith remit to the Receiver General, on account of the shareholder’s tax liability, 15% from the amount otherwise payable on the redemption, acquisition or cancellation, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the share was, at the time it was issued, a tax deferred cooperative share;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the redeeming entity is the corporation that issued the share, or a person or partnership with whom the corporation does not deal at arm’s length; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the shareholder is not a trust whose taxable income is exempt from tax under this Part because of paragraph 149(1)(r) or (x).

Subsection.Text: Section 135.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Subsection (10) applies in respect of the disposition, after September 28, 2009, by a taxpayer of a tax deferred cooperative share (in this subsection and subsection (10) referred to as the “old share”) of an agricultural cooperative corporation if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disposition results from the acquisition, cancellation or redemption of the old share in the course of a reorganization of the capital of the corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in exchange for the old share the corporation issues to the taxpayer a share (in this subsection and subsection (10) referred to as the “new share”) that is described in all of paragraphs (b) to (d) of the definition tax deferred cooperative share in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of paid-up capital, and the amount, if any, that the taxpayer is entitled to receive on a redemption, acquisition or cancellation, of the new share are equal to those amounts, respectively, in respect of the old share.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of an exchange of a taxpayer’s old share for a new share, for the purposes of this section (other than subsection (9)),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the new share issued in exchange for the old share is deemed to have been issued, pursuant to an allocation in proportion to patronage, at the time the old share was issued; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provided that no person or partnership receives at any time any consideration (other than the new share) in exchange for the old share, for the purposes of subsections (2) and (7) the taxpayer is deemed to have disposed of the old share for nil proceeds.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to redemptions, acquisitions and cancellations that occur after 2007.

Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on September 29, 2009, except that in its application to an exchange of shares described by subparagraph 87(2)(s)(ii) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 223(8), that occurs before October 31, 2011,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to a new share received on the exchange that has been disposed of before October 31, 2011, paragraph 135.1(10)(a) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the new share issued in exchange for the old share is deemed to have been issued at the time the old share was issued; and

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 135.1(10)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (2) and (7) the taxpayer is deemed to have disposed of the old share for nil proceeds.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 136(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, a cooperative corporation that would, if this Act were read without reference to this section, be a private corporation is deemed not to be a private corporation except for the purposes of sections 15.1, 123.4, 125, 125.1, 127, 127.1, 152 and 157, the definition mark-to-market property in subsection 142.2(1) and the definition small business corporation in subsection 248(1) as it applies for the purpose of paragraph 39(1)(c).
Subsection 136(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by replacing paragraph (c) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: at least 90% of its members are individuals, other cooperative corporations, or corporations or partnerships that carry on the business of farming; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: at least 90% of its shares, if any, are held by members described in paragraph (c) or by trusts governed by registered retirement savings plans, registered retirement income funds, TFSAs or registered education savings plans, the annuitants, holders or subscribers under which are members described in that paragraph.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2001 and subsequent taxation years.

Subsection (2) applies to the 1998 and subsequent taxation years, except that, in its application to taxation years that end before 2009, paragraph 136(2)(d) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: at least 90% of its shares, if any, are held by members described in paragraph (c) or by trusts governed by registered retirement savings plans, registered retirement income funds or registered education savings plans, the annuitants or subscribers under which are members described in that paragraph.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 137(4.3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the preferred-rate amount of a corporation at the end of a taxation year is an amount equal to the total of its preferred-rate amount at the end of its immediately preceding taxation year and 100/17 of the amount deductible under section 125 from the tax for the year otherwise payable by it under this Part;

Subsection.Text: The definition member in subsection 137(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: member, of a credit union, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is recorded as a member on the records of the credit union and is entitled to participate in and use the services of the credit union, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a registered retirement savings plan, a registered retirement income fund, a TFSA or a registered education savings plan, the annuitant, holder or subscriber under which is a person described in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 137(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, a credit union that would, if this Act were read without reference to this section, be a private corporation is deemed not to be a private corporation except for the purposes of sections 123.1, 123.4, 125, 127, 127.1, 152 and 157 and the definition small business corporation in subsection 248(1) as it applies for the purpose of paragraph 39(1)(c).

Subsection (1) applies to the 2008 and subsequent taxation years, except that, in the application of paragraph 137(4.3) of the Act, as amended by subsection (1), to a particular taxation year of a credit union that began in 2007 and ended in 2008, the preferred-rate amount of the credit union at the end of the particular taxation year is equal to the total of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the preferred-rate amount of the credit union at the end of the taxation year that immediately preceded the particular taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that proportion of the amount obtained by multiplying 25/4 by the amount deductible under section 125 of the Act for the particular taxation year, that the number of days in the particular taxation year that are in 2007 is of the number of days in the particular taxation year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that proportion of the amount obtained by multiplying 100/17 by the amount deductible under section 125 of the Act for the particular taxation year, that the number of days in the particular taxation year that are in 2008 is of the number of days in the particular taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to the 1996 and subsequent taxation years except that, in its application to taxation years that end before 2009, paragraph (b) of the definition member in subsection 137(6) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a registered retirement savings plan, a registered retirement income fund or a registered education savings plan, the annuitant or subscriber under which is a person described in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (3) applies to the 2001 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 137.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following amounts shall not be included in computing the income of a deposit insurance corporation for a taxation year:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any premium or assessment received, or receivable, by the corporation in the year from a member institution; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount received by the corporation in the year from another deposit insurance corporation to the extent that that amount can reasonably be considered to have been paid out of amounts referred to in paragraph (a) received by that other deposit insurance corporation in any taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 137.1(4) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any amount paid by it to another deposit insurance corporation that is, because of paragraph (2)(b), not included in computing the income of that other deposit insurance corporation; or

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to the 1998 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 138(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a life insurer resident in Canada carries on an insurance business in Canada and in a country other than Canada in a taxation year, its income or loss for the year from carrying on an insurance business is the amount of its income or loss for the taxation year from carrying on the insurance business in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a life insurer resident in Canada carries on an insurance business in Canada and in a country other than Canada in a taxation year, for greater certainty,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in computing the insurer’s income or loss for the taxation year from the insurance business carried on by it in Canada, no amount is to be included in respect of the insurer’s gross investment revenue for the taxation year derived from property used or held by it in the course of carrying on an insurance business that is not designated insurance property for the taxation year of the insurer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
